task.description: Repair active_skus so it returns unique active item SKUs sorted by normalized sku. Read the sku from sku or product.sku, trim it, and skip missing, blank, None, or malformed SKU sources. Compare status case-insensitively. Active status tokens are 'active', 'enabled', '1', and True; missing or blank status is inactive. For duplicate normalized SKUs, choose the status row by source priority first (catalog > feed > manual > missing/other), then by greatest numeric updated_at timestamp, then by later input order. Include only SKUs whose winning row is active.

task.description: Repair totals_by_customer so it returns total order amount by customer id while preserving first-seen canonical customer order. Read the customer from customer_id, customer.id, or customer, trimming string ids and skipping blank or missing customers. If order_id is present, duplicate order_ids are revisions: keep only the row with the greatest numeric updated_at timestamp, breaking timestamp ties by later input order, before applying status and totals. Ignore cancelled or void statuses after revision selection and after trimming and case-normalizing status values. Parse amounts from ints, floats, and numeric strings; missing, blank, or None amount is 0.

Repair normalize_contacts so it returns contacts sorted by id with the exact keys id, name, email, and active. Accept email from email, email_address, or contact.email. Trim names and emails, title-case names, lowercase emails, and use explicit active tokens where 'active', 'enabled', 'yes', 'true', '1', and True are active while 'inactive', 'disabled', 'no', 'false', '0', False, missing, blank, or unknown tokens are inactive. Merge duplicate ids by choosing each nonblank name and email field from the greatest source priority first (manual > crm > import > missing/other), then greatest numeric updated_at timestamp, then later input order; blank name/email fields do not replace existing nonblank fields. Choose active by the same priority, timestamp, and input-order rule. Rows with status deleted/remove or deleted set to True are tombstones; if the winning active/status row for an id is a tombstone, omit that contact.

Repair normalize_flags so it returns a dict of boolean flags. Read the flag key from key or name, strip and lowercase it, and skip blank keys. Values 'true', 'yes', 'on', '1', and True mean True; 'false', 'no', 'off', '0', False, missing, blank, None, or unknown tokens mean False. Later rows override earlier rows for the same normalized key.

Repair unique_tags so it returns normalized unique tags in first-seen order. Accept tag values as strings or dicts with tag or label, split comma-separated tag strings, trim whitespace, lowercase tags, collapse internal whitespace, and skip blanks, None, and non-string values.
